title: CBSE relaxes three language policy for Class 7, 8, 9; allows two foreign language options
content: The current batch of class ten will not have to follow the new language policy, the education board stated

title: Kerala CM seeks West Bengal counterpart’s intervention to restore journalist R. Rajagopal’s passport
content: V.D. Satheesan tells Suvendu Adhikari that an adverse police verification report by Kolkata police had hobbled passport renewal process of the former editor of ‘The Telegraph’

title: TET paper leak: Opposition legislators protest, walk out of Maharashtra Assembly
content: Senior leaders alleged that those behind the TET and NEET leaks are linked to the BJP, and demanded a comprehensive investigation into the examination process

title: Teachers, parents of CBSE students worry over lack of clarity on three-language policy in Tamil Nadu
content: Parents of Class IX and X students are frustrated with the lack of direction from CBSE and school authorities as students have to make changes to their second language

title: West Bengal CM says action will be taken against MLA Humayun Kabir for provocative remarks
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that three cases have been registered against the MLA- two at Rejinagar police station and one at Shaktipur police station.

title: ‘Rape’ of Dalit woman: Kerala HC denies bail to ex-Palakkad councillor
content: The former Congress councillor’s anticipatory bail plea was rejected earlier, leading to his dismissal from the party

title: Former Kolkata Councillor Shams Iqbal Anil arrested in ₹70 lakh extortion case
content: According to police sources, with this arrest, the number of Kolkata Councillors arrested in different criminal cases has risen to 10

title: Sikh MLAs, Cabinet Ministers appear before Akal Takht over anti-sacrilege law
content: Non-Sikh Cabinet Ministers were asked to submit their views in writing on the matter, whereas Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann was not summoned

title: SIR: Citizens, citizenship, and Right to Vote
content: The door-to-door survey for Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls begins in Karnataka on June 30, amid a debate about, not the need for the process itself, but how it is going to be carried out

title: Nine golden jackals found electrocuted near Kochi airport
content: The animals are suspected to have died from electric shock caused by a snapped Kerala State Electricity Board power line

title: Priyank Kharge vows legal fight, renews demand for RSS registration after Bengaluru court summons
content: “Where are its donations coming from? Who is maintaining the accounts? Who is benefiting from it and who is losing because of it? All of that should become known,” Mr. Kharge added

title: Kerala Plus One seat shortage: Govt plans 120 more temporary batches in four Malabar districts
content: Proposal likely to come up in next Cabinet meeting. Minister for General Education N. Samsudheen tells Assembly that district and taluk-level scrutiny showed that more Plus One seats would be required in these districts

title: Jammu and Kashmir LG performs ‘Pratham Puja’ to mark ceremonial start of Amarnath Yatra
content: Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha also urged the devotees of Baba Barfani to join the pilgrimage in great numbers and experience the spiritual journey

title: Ram Temple embezzlement row: Supreme Court urged to secure digital evidence before it is ‘quietly lost’
content: During the oral mention, Mr. Goswami informed the Bench that the Supreme Court Registry had informed him that the petition would be taken up only after the summer vacation, which ends on July 12

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Three dead in Arunachal floods; IMD says monsoon in north India may arrive in 5-6 days
content: Intense overnight showers lashed Mumbai’s suburbs on June 28, over 100 mm rain recorded in several areas

title: Why are States opposing VB-G RAM G | Explained
content: While the Bill increases the number of guaranteed workdays from 100 to 125, it also significantly raises the financial burden on States from the current 10% share to 40% of total expenditure

title: Tiruppur woman dies after botched YouTube-assisted childbirth at home
content: K. Sasikala, of Punjaithalavaipalayam in Uthukuli taluk, was taken to a private hospital in Coimbatore, where she died on Sunday morning of post-partum haemorrhage and related complications

title: Another setback for AIADMK as M.R. Vijayabhaskar resigns as MLA in Tamil Nadu
content: Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar accepts the resignation of the Karur constituency legislator. Mr. Vijayabhaskar is the sixth AIADMK MLA to resign months after the Assembly elections.

title: Kerala Assembly: Opposition LDF marks protest over non-disbursal of benefits under Sthree Suraksha Scheme
content: Although Speaker disallowed its notice for an adjournment motion, LDF chose to continue to participate in House proceedings after Pinarayi Vijayan formally registered the Opposition’s protest, contrary to the usual practice of staging a walkout

title: TET paper leak case: Maharashtra Education Minister must resign, says Youth Congress; holds protest
content: Led by Mumbai Youth Congress president Zeenat Shabreen, the protesters raised slogans demanding the Minister's resignation on moral grounds

title: ‘Not just system failure, but blow on dreams of millions’, says Rahul Gandhi on paper leaks, cancelled exams
content: One thing I want to say to every young person in this country — today, in this nation, it's not the fruit of hard work that you get, but punishment for daring to dream, says Congress leader Rahul Gandhi

title: Police lodge FIR after Shia groups clash during Muharram procession in J&K’s Budgam
content: The clashes broke out on Sunday between the followers of the Jammu and Kashmir Shia Association, headed by former Minister Imran Ansari, and those of Maulana Masroor Abbas Ansari during a Muharram procession in the Hardpanzoo area

title: CM Sukhu carries ‘Sarkar Gaon Ke Dwar’ to Himachal’s most inaccessible region, announces key benefits for Bara Bhangal
content: The Chief Minister announced a monthly pension of ₹1,500 for all eligible women in Bara Bhangal and said the region would be developed into an eco-tourism destination.

title: Four held in Ujjain for blowing up car in Muharram stunt; police rule out communal angle
content: Viral videos show a white van hanging through a crane about 40 feet from the ground which is blown up with firecrackers kept inside it, creating a significant explosion

title: Bidadi project provides ground for Vokkaliga leaders’ one-upmanship
content: The political narrative has become messier with personal allegations going back and forth between Shivakumar and Kumaraswamy

title: Fire breaks out at Noida complex, 15 people rescued; short circuit suspected
content: Chief Fire Officer Pradeep Kumar said the fire was reported at a complex near Shatabdi Vihar under Sector 24 police station limits

title: Fire destroys 36 structures housing Central Vista workers
content: Fire officials said the incident was initially attended by two water tenders, but the scale of the blaze led to upgrades, with 19 fire-fighting vehicles, including water tenders, water bowsers and other specialised units, being pressed into service

title: Rabri Devi begins vacating 10, Circular Road, bungalow as deadline ends on June 29
content: The developments came as the bungalow at 10 Circular Road has been allotted to Dairy and Fisheries Minister Nand Kishor Ram

title: Scuffle breaks out among Nihang Sikh groups at Punjab’s Sohana's Gurdwara
content: The incident reportedly happened while the groups were celebrating the return of the four Nihang Sikhs who were granted bail and were brought back to Mohali

title: Nihang-police standoff in Uttarakhand ends after talks, group returns to Himachal
content: The protesters declared they would not return to Punjab until four Nihang members arrested in connection with the Karnprayag incident were released

title: Karnaprayag clash: Arrested Nihang Sikhs may move court for bail
content: According to the sources, if the court grants them bail, the administration may allow them to return to their home State of Punjab

title: Kerala House Protocol Officer suspended after Pinarayi Vijayan stranded at Delhi airport
content: On Saturday, Kerala Opposition Leader arrived at Delhi airport only to discover that the flight booked for him by the protocol office had already departed. As a result, he was stranded for several hours and was forced to take a later flight to reach his home in Kannur

title: In Kerala, breastfeeding figures show a marked improvement
content: As per the National Family Health Survey-6 the proportion of infants under six months who are exclusively breastfed in the State has increased from 55.5% in NFHS-5 to 72.7%. Also, 82.3% of infants are initiated into breastfeeding within an hour of birth, up from 66.7% in NFHS-5. The Mother and Baby-Friendly Hospital Initiative, a certification for hospitals launched in 2021, is a major reason for the improved show

title: Amit Shah dials Assam CM to discuss flood-like situation in parts of State
content: The State government is “mobilising all its resources to prioritise the immediate safety and long-term rehabilitation of all affected families”, he said

title: Landslide dams river in rain-battered Arunachal, warning for Assam downstream
content: Lower Siang district officials say the possibility of a powerful flash flood if the dam breaks could endanger homes along the Siji river, which becomes the Gai river in Assam

title: India broadens its fight against anaemia
content: The emphasis is no longer merely on treating anaemia when it is detected, but on preventing it across every stage of life — even before pregnancy — through a comprehensive lifecycle approach

title: SIT formed to probe Dudhwa tigress’ ‘sudden’ death; NTCA seeks report
content: The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA), the apex body overseeing Project Tiger, has also sought a detailed report on the circumstances leading to the tigress' death

title: SIR exercise must be monitored closely, says Asghar Chulbul
content: He advised citizens to fill up the enumeration form carefully, and obtain an official acknowledgement after submitting it, describing the receipt as an important record of submission

title: AIDSO opposes vocational education in Karnataka Public Schools
content: AIDSO alleged that the proposal is an indirect attempt to implement the National Education Policy (NEP)-2020, which ‘has been rejected by the people of Karnataka’

title: CBSE’s move to make vocational education mandatory for class IX sparks concern among Bengaluru schools
content: CBSE, under its NCF-SE, has introduced Kaushal Vikas (Vocational education) as a mandatory subject for class IX students, rather than keeping it optional

title: I don't have wings, so no question of anybody clipping them: Fadnavis, asserts support of BJP leaders
content: Devendra Fadnavis also dismissed the political buzz triggered after he and former ally-turned-rival, Uddhav Thackeray, travelled on the same flight from Mumbai to Nagpur

title: After the split, mudslinging begins between the newly inducted Shiv Sena MPs and the Shiv Sena(UBT)
content: Sanjay Raut questioned Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis regarding the law and order situation in the State, and dared Sanjay Dina Patil to face him without his police security cover

title: Uddhav calls for ‘BJP-mukt Ram’, breathes fire against the BJP over the Ram temple donations row
content: The Shiv Sena (UBT) chief slams BJP over its stand that a passport is not proof of citizenship, adding that voters need to question the defected MPs as well as the BJP, which wanted to finish off the Opposition

title: Uddhav Thackeray seeks disqualification of rebel MPs, says defections part of bigger conspiracy
content: MPs loyal to the Shiv Sena (UBT) had submitted a formal representation to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, seeking an opportunity to present their side, Uddhav Thackeray said, noting that party MP Arvind Sawant had cut short an official visit to Kargil after the Speaker had advanced the date of the hearing

title: Pune fort murder: Siya’s ‘sitting down’ signal was also meant to ensure her safety, say police
content: Siya Goyal and her alleged lover had decided that she would sit down either to drink water or on the pretext of tying her shoelaces; sitting down was the signal, the official

title: Elephant killings continue in death zone of Kerala
content: Human-induced elephant deaths in the Parambikulam-Munnar–Malayattoor forest sector are back in focus after the death of a makhna elephant in the Malayattoor forests, reportedly after chewing on fruit stuffed with explosives

title: Masterminds? Ram temple donation embezzlement probe focuses on Tinnu Yadav, Anukalp
content: Investigators are zeroing in on Ram Shankar Yadav and Anukalp Mishra as potential masterminds in the Ram Temple donation embezzlement case. Their close ties to senior trust officials and access to the donation system are under scrutiny. Police are examining how Yadav allegedly facilitated his nephew's inclusion and Mishra influenced his brother-in-law's deployment, suggesting a coordinated effort to divert funds.

title: Jilted lover stabs woman to death in Bengaluru after she cut ties with him
content: A tragic incident unfolded in Bengaluru's JC Nagar where a 23-year-old woman, Anjali, was fatally stabbed by her former partner, Rajeev.  Their two-year relationship reportedly ended after Anjali's parents warned her about Rajeev's criminal past.  Furious over being avoided and blocked on her phone, Rajeev allegedly attacked Anjali in her residence late Sunday night.

title: Offside: Can Japan go Kill Bill on Brazil?
content: Will the Dutch trip against Morocco? Can Germany beat Paraguay? Read this and more in today's Offside.

title: 'Expected Sooryavanshi to debut': Gavaskar questions India's call after Ireland setback
content: Sunil Gavaskar expressed surprise at 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's exclusion from the Ireland T20Is, believing he should have debuted before tougher England assignments.  Despite the omission, Gavaskar anticipates Sooryavanshi's eventual call-up, citing his record-breaking domestic feats. Assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate, however, urged patience, emphasising faith in established players while acknowledging the youngster's readiness.

title: On his 55th birthday, Musk has a warning for Sam Altman and Dario Amodei
content: On his 55th birthday, Elon Musk turned to X with a challenge for OpenAI's Sam Altman and Anthropic's Dario Amodei: starting next month, his AI team will ship a brand-new, trained-from-scratch foundation model every month through year's end. First up is Grok 4.5, a 1. 5T-parameter model now in private beta that he claims rivals—or perhaps beats—Anthropic's Claude Opus. A 2T model lands in August.

title: Can OCI footballers play for India? What is a Sports Passport? Complete guide
content: India's football federation is proposing to allow Overseas Citizenship of India (OCI) players in domestic leagues, sparking debate on sporting nationality. While OCIs can play for Indian clubs, they cannot represent the nation internationally without full citizenship. A government 'Sports Passport' proposal aims to potentially change this, drawing parallels with other nations' diaspora talent policies.

title: ‘A man does not die alone’: Chilling messages left by Bangladeshi man before killing family
content: A haunting social media message has surfaced in connection with a horrific triple homicide in Rome, where a father, mother, and their young daughter were discovered lifeless. The primary suspect, 43-year-old Shahadat Hossain from Bangladesh, is the focus of an extensive manhunt by the police. His ominous post, stating, 'A man does not die alone,' is now pivotal to the ongoing investigation.

title: The great economic divide: East-West split could cost world $6.9 trillion, WEF warns
content: The world is witnessing a significant shift from globalization to geo-economic fragmentation, with the World Economic Forum warning of a potential $6.9 trillion hit to global GDP. This fragmentation, already costing billions and increasing inflation, is driven by rising nationalism and trade disputes, particularly between the US and China. Emerging markets face the brunt, while multilateral institutions weaken, and policy uncertainty hampers investment and hiring.

title: Jeff Bezos vs Mamdani debate: Kevin O’Leary says Amazon founder should run cities
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos sparked a debate on government efficiency and taxation, suggesting New York City's services would falter under his company's operational model. Billionaire Kevin O'Leary echoed these sentiments, calling the city a 'disaster' and warning against taxing the wealthy. Former Mayor Bill de Blasio criticized Bezos' remarks as out of touch. Meanwhile, Bezos also dismissed fears of AI causing job losses, predicting a labor shortage instead.

title: 'I hate it... it's unreasonable': Serena calls out anti-doping system ahead of Wimbledon
content: Tennis icon Serena Williams has voiced strong disapproval of the sport's anti-doping system, calling its current testing protocols "unprofessional" and "unreasonable."  Williams revealed these stringent rules nearly deterred her from returning to professional tennis, citing the difficulty of adhering to whereabouts requirements while managing a busy life as a mother and businesswoman.

title: 3 masked men break into Twisha's jailed mother-in-law's Bhopal home, flee with valuables
content: Former district judge Giribala Singh and her advocate son Samarth Singh, currently in judicial custody over Twisha Sharma's suspicious death, were victims of a burglary. Three masked individuals reportedly broke into their Bhopal residence on Saturday night, making off with valuables. Police intercepted suspects near the scene, who allegedly abandoned a stolen bag.

title: Perplexity CEO says this 'Beautiful Mind' habit quietly kills startups
content: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas says his hardest founder lesson was unlearning the academic instinct to perfect an idea on a whiteboard before building. Movies like "A Beautiful Mind" sell a myth of instant genius, he argues, but plans only feel safe—they produce no real information. His fix: ship small experiments fast, watch what users actually do, and iterate. Effort exposed to reality, not polished planning, is what actually teaches you anything.

title: 'Champat Rai, Anil Mishra must leave Ayodhya in 3 days': Lawyers’ body warns of stir amid theft row
content: In a significant move, the Faizabad Bar Association has unanimously resolved not to represent the accused in the Ram temple donation embezzlement case. Lawyers expressed outrage over the alleged theft of temple offerings and warned of a city blockade if key figures do not leave Ayodhya within three days. This stance echoes their historic refusal to defend accused in a 2005 terror attack case.

title: Ben Stokes' retirement: Vaughan claims 'fallout with the ECB' played a key role
content: Former England captain Michael Vaughan speculates that a breakdown in trust between Ben Stokes and the ECB might have contributed to his sudden international retirement. Vaughan observed Stokes seemed "not quite right" even before the Lord's Test, suggesting a loss of confidence in those around him, including the board, as a significant factor alongside other recent challenges.

title: 'No one shall be disadvantaged': CBSE issues three-language policy guidelines, exempts current Class X students
content: CBSE has unveiled new third language policy guidelines under NEP 2020, emphasizing meaningful learning over exams. Students in Class IX from 2026-27 onwards will have internal assessment for the third language, with no board exams. Earlier batches get relaxations, while newer students will face board exams for the third language. Exemptions are provided for CwSN and foreign students, with support for migrating families.

title: ITR filing: Which is the correct tax return form for you? ITR-1 to ITR-7 eligibility explained
content: There are a total of seven income tax return filing forms and each year there are changes in some of these forms, so it’s important to be informed if your ITR form for a financial year is different from the previous year.

title: 'They harass me': Faridabad bizman dies by suicide, blames wife, in-laws in final video
content: A 28-year-old garment trader in Faridabad allegedly died by suicide, posting a video accusing his wife and her family of abuse. Rahul claimed continuous physical and mental harassment, including assault and a false case. His family discovered him after seeing the video. Police have registered a case against the wife and her relatives and are investigating the allegations.

title: Nasa's ageing telescope in space is falling towards Earth. Now a robot is being sent to rescue it
content: NASA is partnering with startup Katalyst Space Technologies to launch a groundbreaking mission to save its aging Swift Gamma-ray burst explorer. This innovative rescue operation, set to begin soon, aims to boost Swift's orbit and extend its scientific life, potentially revolutionizing satellite servicing and paving the way for future missions, including a possible life extension for the Hubble Space Telescope.

title: Workplace harassment law may get stricter checks as NCW calls for annual POSH audits
content: The National Commission for Women is pushing for mandatory annual audits of POSH compliance in workplaces across India, moving beyond mere paperwork. This initiative aims to ensure genuine implementation of the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013, with states like Maharashtra already taking proactive steps. Audits will scrutinize committee functionality, complaint handling, and awareness programs, with non-compliance facing penalties.

title: FIFA WC Round of 32 Schedule: Argentina, France in focus as Canada reach last 16
content: Canada have etched their name in World Cup history with a dramatic stoppage-time victory, propelling them into the Round of 16 stage. This historic win marks their first-ever appearance in the Round of 16. The tournament's expanded format has seen a record-breaking group stage, with nine African nations advancing. The intense Round of 32 matches are underway, leading to a thrilling weekend of sudden-death football.

title: 'Sooryavanshi is absolutely ready, but...': India coach urges patience with teen prodigy
content: India's assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate believes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is ready for international cricket, despite his omission from the Ireland tour. Following a shock T20I series loss to Ireland, the coach emphasised that Sooryavanshi must still follow the team's established selection process. Ten Doeschate acknowledged the team's struggles with conditions and Ireland's superior execution.

title: Three Indian-origin men jailed in London for running £174,000 drugs network
content: In a significant crackdown, authorities have dismantled a large drug trafficking operation in London, orchestrated by incarcerated dealer Hardeep Thind. Three men of Indian descent, including an active magistrate, received prison sentences for their involvement in distributing cocaine and heroin worth £174,000. Following Thind's release, the illegal network expanded, prompting police to conduct thorough investigations, resulting in numerous arrests and convictions.

title: Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike enters day 2 at Jantar Mantar
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk has entered the second day of his indefinite hunger strike at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, joining the Cockroach Janata Party's protest demanding the education minister's resignation over examination irregularities. Allegations of police denying a portable toilet and public facilities lacking water have surfaced. The agitation, supported by students and farmers, began on June 20, with Wangchuk commencing his fast on Sunday after visiting Rajghat.

title: Suhrawardy’s many afterlives: From Direct Action Day to Jordan’s royal family
content: The row over Kolkata’s Suhrawardy Avenue opens a wider story of memory, guilt and legacy. Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy remains tied to Bengal famine, Direct Action Day and Partition. Yet his family’s afterlives stretch across scholarship, diplomacy, human rights, Pakistan’s politics, Bangladesh’s public life and Jordan’s royalty over two turbulent centuries.

title: Gold price prediction: Will prices continue to fall? Key levels to watch this week
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices may find support in the Rs 142,800–143,200 band. They are trading with a bearish bias in the near-term, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: Where is Avesh Khan? The lanky pacer eyes India comeback via Ranji Trophy
content: After a year-long battle with injury and knee surgery, Avesh Khan says he is ready to return to the grind. Fresh off a successful Madhya Pradesh Premier League campaign, the fast bowler speaks about his rehab, Test cricket ambitions, preparing for the Ranji Trophy, and why he sees high-scoring T20s as an opportunity rather than a challenge.

title: Builder ordered to refund buyer who paid Rs 25 lakh in 1995 but got no property
content: A Maharashtra consumer commission has ordered a developer to refund Rs 25 lakh collected in 1995 for 11 commercial units that were never built. The buyer, who intended to use the shops for his business, waited over three decades for possession. The commission found the builder guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice, awarding the buyer Rs 2 lakh for mental agony and Rs 25,000 for litigation costs.

title: 'How is this a blatant lie?' Vedant Shrivastava, CBSE at odds again over revaluation
content: A dispute has erupted between CBSE and student Vedant Shrivastava over his Class 12 marks. While the Board claims a 11-mark increase post-re-evaluation, Vedant insists a 9-mark jump in Physics occurred due to an earlier answer sheet correction, with re-evaluation adding only two marks. This disagreement highlights ongoing concerns about CBSE's new On-Screen Marking system

title: 6 financial rejigs from July 1: What changes for ITR, Aadhaar, passport fees and bank rules
content: Starting July 1, 2026, several financial changes will affect you. Income tax filing deadlines are set, and updating your Aadhaar email becomes free for a period. Expect shifts in SBI Card rewards and HDFC Bank credit card lounge access eligibility. Passport fees are rising, and new RBI rules will protect bank customers from mis-sold financial products, ensuring refunds and compensation.

title: Stalin's son-in-law issues notices against Tamil Nadu ministers over corruption allegations
content: M.K. Stalin's son-in-law, V. Sabareesan, has sent legal notices to two Tamil Nadu ministers, demanding an unconditional apology and retraction of corruption allegations. The ministers are accused of making baseless and defamatory statements against Sabareesan, aiming to tarnish his reputation and that of the former Chief Minister. Failure to comply within 15 days will result in civil and criminal proceedings.

title: Farmhouse, SUV, bike — and Rs 15K salary: Ram temple donation theft accused’s assets under scanner
content: Authorities are investigating Ram Temple donation theft accused Anukalp Mishra for amassing assets potentially disproportionate to his income. Investigators are scrutinizing a newly built farmhouse, a Rs 65 lakh Ayodhya house, and an attempted SUV purchase. Property documents recovered from his residence are under examination, alongside bank records and financial transactions, to determine the source of funds for these acquisitions and other recent purchases.

title: Pune court awards death penalty to 65-year-old for rape, murder of 3-year-old girl
content: A Pune court has handed down the death penalty to Bhimrao Kamble for the brutal kidnapping, rape, and murder of a three-year-old girl. The special fast-track trial, ordered by the Chief Minister, concluded swiftly, with the judge deeming the crime "rarest of rare." CCTV footage was crucial in proving Kamble lured the child to a cowshed where the heinous act occurred.

title: 'Awarapan 2' teaser: Emraan is back as Shivam Pandit; says, 'Dard se purana rishta hai mera'
content: In an exciting return, Emraan Hashmi reprises his role as Shivam Pandit in 'Awarapan 2,' the sequel to the beloved 2007 movie. The recently released teaser on June 29, 2026, marks 19 years since the original's success. Featuring Disha Patani and Shabana Azmi, this film is set to deliver gripping moments and memorable dialogues. Audiences can catch 'Awarapan 2' in theaters on August 14, 2026.

title: Haryana, Rajasthan sign MoU to implement 1994 Upper Yamuna water-sharing pact
content: Haryana and Rajasthan have inked a crucial Memorandum of Understanding in New Delhi to operationalize the 1994 Upper Yamuna River Board Agreement.  Presided over by Union Home Minister Amit Shah, the pact aims to streamline the sharing and management of Yamuna river waters between the two states. This significant step brings a decades-old water-sharing pact closer to full implementation.

title: Agni deters, Pralay disrupts, BrahMos strikes: India’s missile power explained
content: India's missile prowess is on full display, from strategic Agni deterrents to the tactical Pralay and the supersonic BrahMos. These systems, developed through decades of innovation, showcase India's advanced capabilities. Agni ensures nuclear deterrence, BrahMos provides precision conventional strikes, and Pralay fills a crucial tactical gap, collectively bolstering India's defense posture and global standing.

title: FIFA WC: Cape Verde captain Ryan Mendes under investigation for rape allegation
content: Cape Verde's captain, Ryan Mendes, is under investigation by New Zealand Police following an alleged rape by a Brazilian interpreter during the FIFA Series in March 2026. The woman claims Mendes entered her hotel room without permission and assaulted her. Police are actively investigating, reviewing CCTV and forensic evidence. FIFA has stated it takes such allegations seriously and is cooperating with authorities.

title: The iPhone turns 19: Still the gadget that changed modern life
content: Nineteen years ago, the iPhone quietly launched, a device that redefined mobile technology and became the 21st century's most influential consumer product. It integrated cameras, music players, and countless other gadgets into one device, transforming how we communicate, work, and live. This unassuming phone made computing ambient, accessible everywhere, and paved the way for the AI revolution, making revolutionary tech feel routine.

title: Andhra Home Minister Hosts Traditional Baby Shower For Pregnant Woman Cop
content: She enquired about her health and well-being then took part in the traditional ceremony, praying for a safe delivery and good health for both the mother and the baby.

title: A Small Plane Hit Beijing's Tallest Skyscraper. Then The Internet Went Quiet
content: The incident has brought back focus on China's strict control over public information after major events.

title: Bengaluru Man Invites Girlfriend For Dinner, Then Stabs Her To Death
content: Anjali and the accused, Rajeev, had been in a relationship for the past three to four years after meeting at their workplace. However, Anjali's family had opposed their marriage due to Rajeev's background.

title: Siya Goyal Took 1 Crore From Fiance For Wedding Shopping, Gave It To Lover: Sources
content: Ketan Agarwal gave Siya Goyal Rs 1 crore for wedding preparations. Instead of using it for that purpose, Goyal handed the entire sum to her lover Chetan Chaudhary to help him stabilise his career and business.

title: 'She Was Forced Into Car': Cab Driver Who Drove Siya Goyal To Mumbai Airport
content: Pune Murder Probe: The driver, Vaibhav Jadhav, claimed that Siya Goyal's brother, Sahil, held her hand and forcibly made her sit inside the vehicle after they were picked up from Pune.

title: Telangana School Principal Attacked During Protest Over Urdu Classes
content: In his clarification, principal Aamir Khan said that Urdu classes were held in the school only for two days following requests from some parents.

title: Another MLA Resigns From AIADMK, 6th Resignation Since Tamil Nadu Polls
content: An MLA from Karur, Vijayabhaskar met Speaker JCD Prabhakar at the state secretariat this morning and tendered his resignation.

title: Opinion: Opinion | India's Solar Dreams Have A Big China Problem
content: The entire edifice of Indian solar, the panels, the parks, the rooftop schemes, the 500 GW of non-fossil capacity promised by 2030, rests, at its base, on a single supply line that runs across a contested border.

title: Video: Fire At High Rise In Noida, AC blast Suspected
content: Upon receiving information, around half a dozen fire tenders rushed to the spot and firefighting operations were immediately launched.

title: 'Secret Call', Deleted Chats: New Findings In Ketan Agarwal Murder Probe
content: The revelation followed an in-depth forensic examination of the mobile handsets of Goyal and Chaudhary, sources said.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Beyond Summons: Understanding The Authority Of Akal Takht
content: The development comes amid a separate controversy over an objectionable video allegedly linked to CM Mann, an allegation he denies.

title: How Delhi Built Its Most Beloved Hubs On A Fire Trap
content: Delhi fire: Since 2019, 543 people have lost their lives in the city due to fire tragedies. Tracing back to 2016, the figure rises to more than 800, according to Delhi government data. In the first half of 2026 alone, 65 deaths in fire accidents have been reported.

title: Pakistan's 'Double-Tap' Strikes On Afghanistan Border Kill 35, Including Children
content: A "double tap" is a military term used to describe a deliberate second strike on the same location shortly after an initial attack.

title: The Real Cost Of Glass Skin: Inside India's K-Beauty Obsession
content: Dermats say it's an economic irony that Indian consumers pay premium prices for Korean beauty products that don't work in India's tropical climate.

title: Invisible AI Tax: Is An Algorithm Secretly Deciding How Much You Pay?
content: For decades, businesses have relied on dynamic pricing. They routinely raise prices when demand spikes. But AI has taken this a step further.

title: Bought house in a land acquisition zone? Here's what the law says
